meta.title: G90/G99 M5 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW M5 G90 saloon & G99 Touring engine — 4.4-litre S68 V8 PHEV, 727 PS. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the first electrified M5. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW M specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M5 G90/G99 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S68 engine for the G90/G99 M5 typically costs £8,000–£15,000 supply only, depending on variant — the saloon sits at £8,000–£14,000, while the Touring runs £8,500–£15,000. Fitting adds £1,040–£1,430. The plug-in hybrid system adds significant complexity to any engine work. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which G90/G99 M5 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: All G90 and G99 M5s use the same S68B44A plug-in hybrid V8 powertrain. The engine is new, so long-term reliability data is limited, but early indications suggest it's robust. The main concerns are the complexity of the hybrid system and the sheer weight's impact on component wear. BMW's 8-year/100,000-mile battery warranty provides some peace of mind.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the G90/G99 M5?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G90/G99 is still new, but early reports suggest the main concerns are hybrid system complexity, cooling system leaks, and weight-related component wear (tyres, brakes, suspension). The S68 is a new engine; long-term reliability patterns are still emerging.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M5 G90/G99 reliable overall?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early indications suggest yes — the S68 V8 is a robust evolution of the S63, and BMW's hybrid technology is mature. However, the sheer complexity and weight of the car mean maintenance costs will be significant. Extended warranty is strongly recommended.

mainEntity.name: Which G90/G99 M5 variant should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No avoidance needed — both the saloon (G90) and Touring (G99) share the identical powertrain. The choice is purely about body style preference. The Touring is heavier (2,475–2,508 kg vs 2,435 kg) and may have slightly higher running costs.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G90/G99 M5 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Warning signs include reduced electric range (battery degradation), check engine lights related to the hybrid system, overheating, or unusual noises from the V8. The high-voltage battery should be checked regularly; any significant drop in EV range warrants inspection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a G90/G99 M5 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a £110,000+ M5, an £8,000–£14,000 recon is 7–13% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even against a high-mileage £80,000 example, the same recon is 10–18%. The G90/G99 M5 retains value well; engine replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: How long do G90/G99 M5 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S68 V8 is expected to exceed 100,000 miles with proper maintenance, though the hybrid system's battery will degrade over time. BMW's 8-year/100,000-mile battery warranty covers the high-voltage battery. Regular servicing of both the combustion engine and hybrid system is essential.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G90 and G99 M5?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G90 is the four-door M5 saloon (2024–present), while the G99 is the five-door M5 Touring estate (2024–present). Both share the same S68 plug-in hybrid V8 powertrain and M xDrive all-wheel drive. The Touring offers significantly more cargo space (500 litres vs 467 litres) and is heavier (2,475–2,508 kg vs 2,435 kg). Performance is near-identical — 0-60 mph in 3.4 seconds for the saloon, 3.5 seconds for the Touring.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my G90/G99 M5 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation — all G90 and G99 M5s use the S68B44A engine code. This is a 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 producing 577 hp (430 kW) on its own, paired with a 194 hp (145 kW) electric motor for 727 PS (717 hp) system output. Tell us your variant and registration year and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW M specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW M5 G90 & G99 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the seventh-generation M5 G90 saloon and G99 Touring — from the 4.4-litre S68 twin-turbo V8 to the plug-in hybrid system delivering 717 hp. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the first electrified M5 in history. Backed by genuine UK specialist data for 2025.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW M5 G90 & G99 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the M5 G90 saloon and G99 Touring specifically — powered exclusively by BMW's S68 plug-in hybrid V8 powertrain.

engines.power: 430 kW (585 PS) ICE + 145 kW (197 PS) electric = 535 kW (727 PS / 717 hp) system

overview.intro: The seventh-generation BMW M5 (codenamed G90 for the saloon, G99 for the Touring estate) represents a historic shift for BMW's iconic super saloon. Unveiled in June 2024 and entering production in July 2024, it is the first M5 ever to feature a plug-in hybrid powertrain. The G90/G99 M5 is built on the G60 5 Series platform and marks the return of the M5 Touring to markets including the UK and US for the first time since the E61. Power comes exclusively from BMW's S68 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 paired with an electric motor, delivering 727 PS and 1,000 Nm of torque through an eight-speed M Steptronic transmission and M xDrive all-wheel drive.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW G60 (CLAR) • Predecessor: BMW M5 F90 • Successor: Current (in production) • Sibling body styles in this generation: G90 Saloon, G99 Touring.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G90/G99 M5 generates steady UK specialist enquiries as the first electrified M5 enters the performance car market.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — one paragraph: The G90 and G99 M5 are powered exclusively by the S68B44A plug-in hybrid V8 powertrain. This 4.4-litre twin-turbo unit produces 577 hp from the combustion engine alone, supplemented by a 194 hp electric motor for a total system output of 727 PS (717 hp) and 1,000 Nm of torque. The S68 is a significant evolution over the S63 it replaces, with the electric motor integrated into the eight-speed automatic transmission. The M5 saloon (G90) accelerates from 0-60 mph in 3.4 seconds; the Touring (G99) follows at 3.5 seconds. The powertrain is praised for its effortless power delivery and duality — silent EV mode for commuting, brutal V8 performance when provoked. However, the plug-in hybrid system adds approximately 400 kg (882 lbs) of weight, bringing the saloon to 2,435 kg and the Touring to 2,475–2,508 kg. The S68 is a new engine, so long-term reliability data is limited — but early indications suggest it's robust, with the main concerns being the complexity of the hybrid system and the sheer weight's impact on component wear. For most buyers, the G90 saloon offers the purest M5 experience; the G99 Touring offers unmatched practicality without sacrificing performance.

ownershipEconomics.h2: BMW M5 G90 & G99 — Ownership Economics

economicsRule.title: The G90/G99 rule
economicsRule.text: An £8,000–£14,000 S68 recon against a £110,000+ M5 sits at 7–13% of the car's value — within our 70% BMW threshold. Even against a high-mileage £80,000 example, the same recon is 10–18% — a significant financial decision but well within the car's value. The G90/G99 M5 is a premium performance vehicle; engine replacement is almost always viable given the car's retained value. However, the S68's hybrid complexity means specialist maintenance is essential.

problems.title: S68 Hybrid System Complexity
problems.rootCause: The S68 is a new, complex plug-in hybrid system combining a twin-turbo V8 with a 145 kW electric motor, 18.6 kWh battery pack, and sophisticated cooling and charging systems. The sheer complexity of the system means more potential failure points — battery degradation, cooling system leaks, charging system faults, and software issues are all concerns. The battery pack alone adds significant weight and complexity.
problems.recommendation: If you own or are buying a G90/G99 M5, have the hybrid system and battery health checked regularly. The 18.6 kWh battery is a significant investment — look for any signs of reduced electric range or charging issues. BMW's warranty should cover the battery for 8 years/100,000 miles, but extended warranty is strongly recommended.

cta.label: Compare S68 engine prices for your M5 →

problems.title: S68 Cooling System
problems.rootCause: The S68's twin-turbo V8 generates significant heat, and the cooling system is under constant stress. The hybrid system also requires additional cooling for the battery and electric motor. Cooling system leaks — from the radiator, water pump, and hoses — are a known concern on high-performance BMWs.
problems.recommendation: On any G90/G99 M5, have the cooling system inspected regularly — a £1,500–£3,000 preventative repair is cheaper than the £8,000+ replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare S68 engine prices for your M5 →

problems.title: Weight-Related Component Wear
problems.rootCause: The G90/G99 M5 weighs approximately 2,435 kg (saloon) to 2,508 kg (Touring) — nearly 1,000 lbs heavier than the outgoing F90. This significant weight places additional stress on suspension components, brakes, tires, and drivetrain components. Tyre wear, brake pad wear, and suspension bushings will all require more frequent replacement.
problems.recommendation: Budget for increased running costs — tyres, brakes and suspension components will wear faster than on lighter vehicles. Regular inspections are essential.

replacementCosts.h2: G90/G99 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Engine Page baseline — no dedicated Engine Page exists for the S68 family yet, so baseline figures are drawn from the EM_Proprietary_Data Table A. Labour hours estimated at 16–22 hours for the S68 engine due to the complexity of the twin-turbo V8 and plug-in hybrid system. The G99 Touring may require slightly more labour due to body configuration differences.

coreVariants.scopeNote: Both variants are powered exclusively by the S68B44A 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 plug-in hybrid powertrain producing 727 PS. The G90 is the four-door saloon; the G99 is the five-door Touring estate. Both feature M xDrive all-wheel drive with 2WD mode available. The M5 Competition is expected to follow with increased power, but is not yet confirmed.
